mechanism_narrative: Monthly acceleration in industrial production signals expanding corporate revenues and operating leverage, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the top quintile of INDPRO momentum fails to outperform the bottom quintile.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9 percent signals severe labor market slack that suppresses wage income and consumption expenditures, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered months when UNRATE exceeds 9 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously low federal funds rates and a rising consumer price index generate negative real interest rates that drive capital reallocation from bonds to equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal fails to outperform single-factor models.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts during early economic expansion signal improving construction activity and household formation, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases in early expansion periods are followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 45% of instances, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP signals broad-based aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate sales and profit expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 surprises do not correlate with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns, or the walk-forward Rank IC drops below 0.03 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5 percent signals restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and debt service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5 percent, or the triggered hit rate is below 55% for negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in industrial production and payroll employment signals coordinated supply and demand expansion that validates inventory investment and capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of INDPRO and PAYEMS produces a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year window, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power above single factors.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points over three months signals a turning point in labor market momentum that boosts consumer confidence and spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Trigger instances of UNRATE declines exceeding 0.2pp are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of cases, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is negative.
